title: Cheese Paneer Garlic Dosa
tagline: College-canteen style dosa loaded with garlic chutney, soft seasoned paneer and melty Amul cheese.
servingsNote: 2 large dosas — uses ready dosa batter
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients (2 dosas, 200 g paneer + 2 Amul cubes).
Good protein for a breakfast (~30 g/serving), but butter + cheese push the fat up. Most of the swing is the cooking butter.
Leaner: use less butter around the edges and just 1 cheese cube total instead of 2 — still tastes canteen-legit and drops ~120–150 kcal per dosa.
Next-day protein boost: make it an egg dosa — soft-scramble 3 eggs (stop at ~85–90% cooked, it finishes inside the dosa) and spread over the dosa before folding, adds ~18–20 g protein.

items: Butter or ghee, for cooking (canteen taste = butter)
2 dried red chillies (or 1 tsp red chilli powder)
2 Amul cheese cubes, grated (or mozzarella — Amul is the authentic canteen taste)

steps.title: Soften the paneer (do not skip)
steps.body: Grate all 200 g paneer, put it in a bowl, and pour hot water (~80–90°C, not violently boiling) over it until submerged. Leave 10 minutes, then drain and gently squeeze out excess water. Pre-packaged paneer is rubbery straight from the pack; this hot-water soak rehydrates it soft. Don't fry it first (turns to eraser) or boil it long.

steps.title: Make the garlic chutney
steps.body: Blend/crush 8 garlic cloves, 2 dried red chillies, 1/2 tsp salt and 1–2 tbsp water into a thick paste. Heat 1 tsp oil in a tiny pan and cook the paste 30–45 seconds just to mellow the raw bite. (If you want an aggressive raw-garlic kick like some canteens, skip cooking.) Set aside.

steps.title: Cook the paneer filling
steps.body: Heat 1 tsp butter/oil, add the chopped onion, green chilli and 1 tsp ginger and cook 1–2 minutes until the onion softens — don't brown. Add 1/4 tsp red chilli powder, a pinch of turmeric and very little salt. Add the softened paneer and 2 tbsp milk, mix gently, and cook only 30–60 seconds — you're warming and seasoning, not frying. Finish with 2 tbsp coriander and a pinch of chaat masala. Target texture: soft, moist, creamy crumble.

steps.title: Spread the dosa
steps.body: Heat the tava well — a sprinkle of water should dance instantly; wipe off the excess. Pour a ladle of batter in the centre and spread thin in circles. Add 1 tsp butter around the edges and cook until the top starts drying.

steps.title: Assemble
steps.body: Spread a thin layer of garlic chutney over the dosa. Add half the paneer filling evenly. Top with 1 grated cheese cube. Press lightly.

steps.title: Crisp & fold
steps.body: Cook another 1–2 minutes on medium until the bottom is crisp and the cheese melts. For canteen crisp, press lightly with the spatula and drizzle a tiny bit more butter. Fold and serve. Repeat for the second dosa.

notes: Don't overload the filling or the dosa tears and becomes a sad uttapam hybrid.
For extra canteen creaminess, toss the drained paneer with 1 tbsp curd/milk and a pinch of chaat masala and let it sit 5 minutes before filling.
Optional street-food upgrade: add thin onion slices + capsicum with the paneer.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
